title: Categorical local Langlands and torsion classes of some Shimura varieties

thm:1.1.summary: Categorical decomposition: the compact part $\mathrm{D}_{\mathrm{lis}}^{[C_\phi]}(\mathrm{Bun}_G,\Lambda)^\omega$ of the Fargues–Scholze category along an $L$-parameter $\phi$ decomposes as $\bigoplus_{\chi \in \mathrm{Irr}(S_\phi)} \mathrm{D}(\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(\mathfrak{s}_{\phi,\chi}))$ — a categorical local Langlands equivalence indexed by the centralizer $S_\phi$.

thm:1.2.summary: The sheaf $\mathcal{G}_\phi$ is a non-trivial Hecke eigensheaf with eigenvalue the $L$-parameter $\phi$ — verifying a case of the Fargues eigensheaf conjecture inside the Fargues–Scholze framework.

thm:1.3.summary: For $\mathrm{GL}_n$ and minuscule $\mu$, an explicit identity (up to shifts/twists) for the cohomology $R\Gamma_c(\mathrm{GL}_n,b,\mu)[\pi]$ as an induced $\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{Q}_p) \times W_{\mathbb{Q}_p}$-representation — torsion classes in the cohomology of the corresponding Shimura/Rapoport–Zink spaces.

thm:2.1.summary: Imported from FS (Thm III.0.2): structure of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ — it is a small $v$-stack, its points biject with $B(G) \cong \pi_1(G)_\Gamma$ (Kottwitz), and the Harder–Narasimhan strata give the geometry the categorical correspondence is built on.

thm:4.3.summary: Zelevinsky-type classification (after Mínguez–Sécherre): the map $\mathrm{Z}: \mathrm{Mult}^{\mathrm{sc}}_m \to \mathrm{Irr}(\mathrm{G}_m)$ from multisegments of supercuspidals to irreducibles is a bijection — the representation-theoretic indexing on the automorphic side.

thm:4.5.summary: Block decomposition (Sécherre–Stevens): $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(G_b)$ decomposes into blocks $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(\mathfrak{s})$ over inertial supercuspidal classes — the spectral decomposition matched against the $L$-parameter side.

thm:4.6.summary: Imported from FS (Thm I.9.6): properties of the Fargues–Scholze correspondence $\pi \mapsto \phi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\pi$ — compatibility with reduction mod $\ell$, twists, and central characters — the constraints that pin the categorical decomposition to the correct parameters.

thm:5.2.summary: Supercuspidal case: for each block, the spectral action realizes the categorical equivalence $\mathrm{D}(\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(\mathfrak{s}_{\phi,\chi})) \simeq$ modules over $\mathcal{O}([C_\phi])$, the engine of the main decomposition (Thm 1.1).

edges.note: The categorical decomposition is indexed by the block decomposition of $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(G_b)$ (Thm 4.5).
Matching blocks to $L$-parameters uses the properties of the Fargues–Scholze correspondence (Thm 4.6).
The decomposition lives on $\mathrm{Bun}_G$, whose structure (Thm 2.1) is the geometric stage.
The block-level categorical equivalence (Thm 5.2, supercuspidal case) assembles into the full decomposition.
The Hecke eigensheaf is extracted from the categorical decomposition.
The Shimura/RZ cohomology identity is read off the Hecke-eigensheaf structure.
The cohomology is matched to the correct $L$-parameter via the FS correspondence.
The Zelevinsky classification indexes the blocks.

fs_geometry.description: The Fargues–Scholze geometric inputs: Bun_G structure and the correspondence properties.

representation_side.description: Block decomposition and Zelevinsky classification of the automorphic side.

main_results.description: The categorical decomposition, the Hecke eigensheaf, and the Shimura/RZ cohomology computation.

FS.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
FS.role: Bun_G structure (Thm III.0.2 $\to$ node 2.1) and the FS correspondence properties (Thm I.9.6 $\to$ node 4.6); the categorical decomposition is of the FS category D_lis(Bun_G)

MS14.title: Mínguez–Sécherre, Représentations lisses modulo l de GL_m(D)

SS16.title: Sécherre–Stevens, Block decomposition of smooth representations
SS16.role: block decomposition into inertial supercuspidal classes (Thm 4.5)
